message.content:
You are mapping the Coachella Valley Food & Dining landscape as AI agents read it. Your specific angle: Map La Quinta dining: Old Town La Quinta historic district, La Quinta Resort restaurants, PGA West area, Madison Club outer ring, highway 111 La Quinta strip.

Query the web extensively for this angle. Use WebSearch (load via ToolSearch: query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (load via ToolSearch: query "WebFetch") for live source-reading. Run multiple searches at different framings — vary the wording to surface different result sets.

The Coachella Valley = nine incorporated cities: Palm Springs, Desert Hot Springs, Cathedral City, Rancho Mirage, Palm Desert, Indian Wells, La Quinta, Indio, Coachella — plus adjacent communities (Bermuda Dunes, Thousand Palms, Mecca, Thermal).

For each establishment found, return:
- name (exact, as it appears in primary sources)
- city (one of the nine above, or "Adjacent Communities")
- cuisine_or_type
- why_mentioned (e.g., "consistently appears on Eater best-of list", "James Beard semifinalist 2024", "longtime independent operating since 1985")
- sources (URLs of pages where this surfaced — at least 1, prefer 2-3)

Be inclusive. The map's job is completeness, not curation. Surface:
- Dominant names that appear on best-of lists
- Notable independents
- Longtime / multi-generation places
- Underpowered places that earn their spot by reputation despite weak online presence
- Anchor-reference entities (chains, resort-owned, franchises) where they genuinely shape the landscape

Aim for ~12 entities. Quality of identification matters — downstream dedupe handles overlap.

Discipline: do not invent. If a "James Beard winner" is being claimed, confirm via primary source before listing it. Mention only what you can substantiate.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's information about the restaurants at La Quinta Resort and Club:

## Morgan's in the Desert

Morgan's in the Desert showcases contemporary American cuisine inspired by the bounty of the Coachella Valley. The restaurant sources the region's top products and crafts them with traditional cooking methods to bring you deliciously simple and healthy dishes. The dining room and lounge are open daily from 5pm to 9:30pm, with live entertainment offered Wednesday, Thursday, and Sunday from 5:30pm to 9:30pm, and Friday and Saturday from 6pm to 10pm.

## Adobe Grill

Adobe Grill offers an authentic Mexican dining experience with traditional Oaxacan motif and a double-sided fireplace. The restaurant features talented bartenders who create bespoke margaritas from more than 100 tequilas. Lunch is served Friday through Sunday from 10am to 3pm, dinner is served daily from 5pm to 10pm, happy hour is daily from 3pm to 5pm (bar only), and there is entertainment Thursday through Sunday from 5:30pm to 8:30pm.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best restaurants along Highway 111 in La Quinta:

**Top Restaurant Options:**

Maru, Kiki's, Fisherman's Market & Grill, La Quinta Cliffhouse, Norma's Italian Kitchen, LA Brasserie Bistro & Bar, Let's Eat Rice & Grill, Red Robin Gourmet Burgers and Brews, Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar, and Lola's At The Cove are among the best-rated restaurants in the area.

**Notable Recommendations:**

**La Quinta Cliffhouse** - Jutting from a rock outcropping above the desert floor and surrounded by mountains, the La Quinta Cliffhouse is renowned for its spectacular views across the valley. The classic American menu showcases perfectly executed steak, pasta and fresh seafood dishes, complimented by an award-winning wine list and creative cocktails. Located at 78250 Highway 111, La Quinta, CA.

**La Brasserie Bistro & Bar** - La Brasserie Bistro & Bar is an authentic French bistro that juxtaposes sophistication, charm, and casualness with a high-energy environment. Located at 78477 Highway 111, La Quinta, CA 92253.

**Maru** - This Korean BBQ restaurant has received praise for quality, with "Maru is actually good kbbq in the middle of the desert... better than a lot of those" LA spots.

These restaurants offer a variety of dining experiences from casual to upscale along the Highway 111 strip.

I've found comprehensive information about the best restaurants in Old Town La Quinta's historic district. Here are the highlights:

## Overview
Old Town La Quinta features over 30 restaurants, boutiques, salons, and services in a setting treasured for its architectural charm and village atmosphere. Local chefs are passionate about California cuisine, infusing their creativity into brunch, lunch and dinner menus that feature everything from fresh fish, salads and creative flatbreads to pizza, pasta and steak.

## Featured Restaurants

**Fine Dining & American:**
- The Grill on Main offers a new approach to classic American fare, bringing forth inventive twists on classic dishes.
- Lavender Bistro offers a world class Contemporary American menu with an extensive wine list, and their outdoor patio offers an unsurpassed evening dining experience blooming with fresh flowers, sparkling lights, and live music.

**Historic/Retro Dining:**
- DSRT CLUB pays homage to the OG eatery, Desert Club, that once catered to upscale patrons as a glamorous supper club from the 1930s.

**Mexican & Latin:**
- TQLAS Agave Bar and Grill offers among the largest selection of specialty tequilas, mezcals and signature margaritas in the La Quinta area, with sizzling platters of fajitas and California Burritos.
- A family-owned Latin tapas restaurant with signature dishes including Spanish Paella and Brazilian Steak Skewers.

**Casual Dining:**
- Stuft Pizza Bar and Grill offers pizza and much more, with the option to build your own pizza or choose tried-and-true fan-favorites from the menu.
- Yes Please is a charming cafe perfect for brunch served all day long with bubbly to savor.

Based on the search results, here are the dining options at PGA West in La Quinta:

## Main Dining Venues

The Tournament Clubhouse is home to Ernie's Bar & Grill, which is the most prominent public restaurant at PGA West. You can watch the sunrise over the Nicklaus Tournament Course while sipping mimosas, celebrate your round in this sports bar setting, and enjoy home-style favorites and formal dining complete with wine pairings. The cuisine highlights American classics like chicken wings, burgers, steaks, and nachos, plus pizzas and unique offerings like Spicy Tuna Poke Bowls and Shrimp and Scampi Lemon Pasta. Ernie's features a large outdoor patio with dining tables, lounge-style furniture, gas fire pits and beautiful views of the Stadium and Nicklaus Tournament courses, with outdoor heaters for cooler evenings.

The Bunker Bar offers a contemporary spin on classic California flavors in an inviting setting at the Pete Dye Clubhouse.

Wallaby's West, nestled inside the Greg Norman Clubhouse and open to the public, welcomes both Members and guests with its elevated comfort food and stunning mountain views.

Additional dining options include the Clubhouse Restaurant, VIP Cabanas, and the Cantina, a locals' favorite to watch all the action on the big screen.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Lavender Bistro in La Quinta:

Lavender Bistro was established in 2008, making it a longtime restaurant in the area. It is a family-owned restaurant that has become one of the most coveted reservations in the Coachella Valley.

The restaurant is voted "Best Romantic Restaurant" and specializes in "New American Cuisine" with only the freshest of ingredients sourced from local farms. They serve only the finest, freshest and organic ingredients; from their pastries to their finest meats and freshly caught seafood.

The restaurant features a beautiful setting with live entertainment 7 days a week in their Fireside Lounge and on their patio. Lavender Bistro offers a "beautiful atmosphere" with enchanting outdoor seating under twinkling lights.

Located at 78-073 Calle Barcelona, La Quinta, California, the restaurant is open Monday through Sunday from 4:00 pm to 9:00 pm.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Madison Club La Quinta's dining clubhouse:

## Clubhouse Overview

The Madison Club boasts a private clubhouse that is an architectural marvel offering dining, social spaces, and event venues. The 56,000-square-foot Addison Mizner-inspired clubhouse embodies casual elegance and offers a welcoming atmosphere for members.

## Dining Amenities

Features include a Pro Shop, Bar and Lounge, Main Dining Room with Sushi Bar, Game Room, Private Movie Theater, and Fitness Center. The clubhouse's dining room overlooks the 18th green and offers stunning views of the San Jacinto and Santa Rosa mountain ranges.

Multiple dining options are available on-site, offering everything from casual lunches to gourmet dinners, prepared by top chefs using the finest ingredients.

## Additional Facilities

Men's and Women's Locker Rooms, a Spa, and the Discovery Performance Center are also located within the Clubhouse. There are five clubhouse member suites which provide luxury accommodations for short-term visits by members and their guests.

The Madison Club is an exclusive private community with membership limited to 225 members, making it one of the most prestigious golf and residential communities in La Quinta, California.

Based on the search results, here's information about notable restaurants in La Quinta near Palm Springs:

## Best Restaurants in La Quinta

**Fine Dining Options:**

Morgan's in the Desert showcases contemporary American cuisine inspired by the bounty of the Coachella Valley, sourcing the region's top products and crafting them with traditional cooking methods. This restaurant has been open since 1926 and was originally created as a place for family members and friends to gather together and dine.

Kiki's is an Italian-influenced restaurant helmed by chef Jon Butler, who also manages kitchens at Holiday House and Sparrows Lodge in Palm Springs. The bounty of the harvest shines through in his exciting, seasonal menu at Kiki's.

**Mexican Cuisine:**

Adobe Grill is famed for its award-winning tamales and signature margaritas, offering the authentic regional Mexican dining experience in the Palm Springs area.

**Casual & Modern Options:**

TWENTY6 offers classic comfort food with an imaginative twist, featuring innovative approaches to familiar favorites using locally sourced, seasonal ingredients with a relaxed atmosphere.

La Brasserie Bistro and Bar is the best reviewed restaurant in La Quinta.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Hog's Breath Inn in La Quinta's Old Town:

## Overview
Hog's Breath Inn is located at 78065 Main Street in Old Town La Quinta, CA 92253. Founded in Carmel by legendary actor Clint Eastwood, the La Quinta counterpart offers a more contemporary setting in Old Town.

## History and Location
In 1999, Clint Eastwood asked Kaiser Morcus to reopen the Original Hog, which Eastwood had owned for more than three decades in Carmel. In 2004, when developers of Old Town La Quinta offered Morcus a prime spot on Main Street, he created the Desert Hog location in Southern California.

## Dining Features
The restaurant features a spacious saloon, rustically sophisticated dining room, private boardroom, and sun-drenched patio all offering stellar views of the Santa Rosa Mountains and Old Town La Quinta Plaza below. The warm, Southwestern-themed, second-story eatery won both a Diner's Choice Award from Open Table and a Certificate of Excellence from TripAdvisor in 2013.

## Menu and Service
The restaurant serves mid-to-upper level, all-American classics, and menu items include sandwiches like the balsamic portobello and goat cheese hoagie, along with Hollywood-themed dishes like the "Gran Torino" lobster sandwich and "Dirty Harry" dinner featuring chopped steak with wild mushroom-mustard sauce.

**Note:** The Yelp listing indicates the restaurant is now closed as of April 2026.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Fisherman's Market and Grill in La Quinta:

## Overview
Fisherman's Market & Grill offers authentic seafood made with locally sourced ingredients across three convenient locations: Palm Springs, La Quinta and Palm Desert.

## History & Longevity
The restaurant was established in 2002. Louis Pagano and life-long friend Bill Mortz founded the business 15 years ago (from the Yelp listing dated 2022). The restaurant has been voted the number one seafood restaurant for the last 16 consecutive years.

## Specialties & Recognition
The restaurant is voted best fish tacos, best seafood restaurant, and best value in the Coachella Valley. They are known for salads, fish, halibut, calamari, sandwiches, oysters, grilled chicken, lobster, sweet potato fries, tacos, and various other seafood dishes.

## Sourcing & Quality
They source and transport their seafood themselves in their own trucks every day. Their grilled shrimp skewers started as a weekend family recipe, passed down and perfected over time, with that same sense of tradition living in every item on the menu.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Arnold Palmer's Restaurant in La Quinta and its signature features:

## Signature Feature

The outdoor patio is a signature feature that has been recently remodeled and is praised by guests for its lighting, heaters, live guitar music, palm-tree landscaping and comfortable seating. The extensive newly remodeled patio was awarded 2022 top 100 outdoor dining restaurants in the country by OpenTable diners and offers live entertainment every night featuring Mark Linford with stunning views of the Santa Rosa Mountains and breathtaking landscape.

## Signature Dishes

As for signature menu items, guests frequently call out bacon-wrapped dates, Arnie's famous meatloaf, prime rib, pork chop, branzino, and shrimp scampi ravioli as favorites, with desserts like butter cake, coconut cream pie, and Arnie's banana split also receiving strong praise.

## About the Restaurant

Open since 2004, Arnold Palmer's Restaurant is one of the most popular restaurants in the Coachella Valley. Arnold Palmer's Restaurant features Progressive American cuisine, including fresh seafood, steaks, and some of Arnie's great comfort foods.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Stuft Pizza Bar & Grill in La Quinta:

## Overview
Stuft Pizza® Bar & Grill is located in the heart of Old Town La Quinta, at 78015 Main St, Ste 100, La Quinta, CA 92253.

## Background & Ownership
Jack and Pat Bertram founded the first Stuft Pizza in Cerritos, California in 1976. By 1986, it expanded into a franchise, with the Bertram Family operating two unique locations in the Coachella Valley since 2004 and 2014, and Stuft Pizza remains a beloved family-run establishment.

## Menu & Offerings
They are best known for Gourmet Pizza, Blackened Chicken Pizza, Rib Bites, Street Tacos, Ahi Salad, Baja Fish Tacos, Asiago Mac & Cheese, Fish & Chips, Prime Rib, Burgers and Sandwiches. They offer a full bar and more than 40 wines by the glass.

## Local Reputation
Stuft Pizza is known as a bustling locals' favorite with super-generous portions and a friendly, energetic atmosphere.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Okura Sushi Robata in La Quinta:

## Overview
Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar serves up a fusion of Japanese and California cuisine in a chic contemporary restaurant space that has an L.A./Hollywood vibe.

## Location & Hours
The restaurant is located at 78480 Hwy 111, La Quinta, CA 92253, and is open Monday-Thursday 3:30 pm - 9:00 pm, Friday-Saturday 3:30 pm - 9:30 pm, and Sunday 3:30 pm - 9:00 pm.

## Décor
Custom décor includes gold Kriskadecor chains hanging from the ceiling, a 4-foot high bronze Buddha and a life-size, petrified Japanese cherry blossom tree at the center of the restaurant.

## Seafood Quality
Okura is the only sushi restaurant in the region to receive daily deliveries of Grade A fish and seafood coming in from Japan.

## Menu
Beyond an expansive sushi menu, there is something for everyone including skewers from the robata grill, salads, Kobe steak, tempura and chicken, beef and fish entrees, as well as delicious desserts!

## Bar & Cocktails
The full bar features signature cocktails such as the Geisha Girl and Lychee Martini, as well as sake, wine and beer.

## Contact
Phone: (760) 564-5820

**Location and Contact:**
La Brasserie is located at 78477 Highway 111, La Quinta, CA 92253, and can be reached at (760) 771-4400.

**Chef:**
The restaurant is led by highly experienced and award-winning French chef Emmanuel Janin. Chef/owner and Brittany native Emmanuel Janin offers a seasonal menu using plenty of locally sourced ingredients.

**Restaurant Style:**
La Brasserie Bistro & Bar is an authentic French bistro that juxtaposes sophistication, charm, and casualness with a high-energy environment. The restaurant was established in 2011.

**Menu:**
The menu features fresh and traditional bistro cuisine combined with California-forward cuisine.

content.text: I have enough to assemble a strong map. Compiling the structured output now.

entities.name: Morgan's in the Desert
entities.why_mentioned: Signature fine-dining restaurant at La Quinta Resort & Club (Waldorf Astoria); consistently named on best-of and fine-dining lists for the city; flagship of historic resort dating to 1926

entities.name: Adobe Grill
entities.why_mentioned: Resort-anchor Mexican restaurant at La Quinta Resort & Club with 100+ tequila selection and award-winning tamales; signature Oaxacan-motif room with double-sided fireplace

entities.name: Lavender Bistro
entities.why_mentioned: Family-owned since 2008; repeatedly cited as one of the most coveted reservations in the Coachella Valley; voted Best Romantic Restaurant; signature twinkle-light garden patio with live music nightly

entities.name: Arnold Palmer's Restaurant
entities.why_mentioned: Celebrity/legacy-anchor restaurant open since 2004 named for golf legend Arnold Palmer; OpenTable Top 100 Outdoor Dining (2022); signature comfort dishes (Arnie's meatloaf, bacon-wrapped dates) consistently called out by guests

entities.name: La Quinta Cliffhouse
entities.why_mentioned: Highway 111 landmark perched on a rock outcropping; known for valley views, award-winning wine list; consistently surfaces on La Quinta best-of lists

entities.name: La Brasserie Bistro & Bar
entities.why_mentioned: Authentic French bistro on Highway 111 by Brittany-native chef/owner Emmanuel Janin; opened 2011; frequently cited as one of the best-reviewed restaurants in La Quinta

entities.name: Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar
entities.why_mentioned: Highway 111 sushi destination cited as the only sushi restaurant in the region with daily Grade A deliveries from Japan; distinctive interior (bronze Buddha, petrified cherry blossom tree)

entities.name: Fisherman's Market & Grill
entities.why_mentioned: Founder-operated since 2002 (Louis Pagano and Bill Mortz); Highway 111 location of a three-restaurant Coachella Valley group repeatedly voted best seafood and best fish tacos in the valley

entities.name: Stuft Pizza Bar & Grill
entities.why_mentioned: Old Town La Quinta anchor on Main Street operated by the Bertram family (parent brand founded 1976 in Cerritos; La Quinta unit since 2004); longtime locals' favorite consistently on Old Town dining guides

entities.name: Ernie's Bar & Grill at PGA West
entities.why_mentioned: Primary public-access restaurant at PGA West (Tournament Clubhouse); anchors the PGA West dining ring with patio overlooking Stadium and Nicklaus Tournament courses

entities.name: The Madison Club
entities.cuisine_or_type: Private club dining (main dining room, sushi bar, bar/lounge)
entities.why_mentioned: Outer-ring anchor entity: 56,000 sq ft Addison Mizner-inspired private clubhouse limited to 225 members; main dining room overlooks 18th green with sushi bar, bar/lounge and member-suite dining shaping the high-end La Quinta dining landscape even though private

entities.name: DSRT CLUB (Desert Club)
entities.why_mentioned: Old Town La Quinta supper club explicitly paying homage to the original 1930s Desert Club that catered to early Hollywood-era La Quinta patrons; surfaces in Old Town dining directory as a historic-reference concept

content.text:
La Quinta dining map compiled across the five zones (Old Town, La Quinta Resort, PGA West, Madison Club outer ring, Highway 111 strip) with 12 entities. Key notes on what surfaced:

- **Resort anchor (La Quinta Resort & Club / Waldorf Astoria)**: Morgan's in the Desert and Adobe Grill are the two flagships consistently cited.
- **Old Town historic district**: Stuft Pizza (Bertram family operator since 2004), Lavender Bistro (just outside Old Town proper at Calle Barcelona), and DSRT CLUB as the explicit historic-reference concept honoring the 1930s Desert Club. Hog's Breath Inn (Clint Eastwood) appears closed per Yelp April 2026 so I excluded it.
- **PGA West**: Ernie's Bar & Grill is the dominant public-access anchor; Bunker Bar and Wallaby's West also exist but are more member-facing.
- **Madison Club outer ring**: Included as an anchor-reference private entity given its scale (56,000 sq ft clubhouse, dining room + sushi bar) shapes the high-end landscape even though private.
- **Highway 111 strip**: La Quinta Cliffhouse, La Brasserie (chef Emmanuel Janin), Okura Robata, Fisherman's Market & Grill, and Arnold Palmer's (technically Avenue 52 just off the strip).

No James Beard claims made — none surfaced with primary-source substantiation for La Quinta specifically.
